meaning: n.[逻][法]前提,(企业,机构等使用的)房屋连地基vt.提论,预述,假定vi.作出前提
examples: The premise is that schools will work harder to improve if they must compete.
His reasoning is based on the premise that all people are equally capable of good and evil.
Linda, co-founder of Scores, a leading digital analytic company, established her organization on a simple premise.
Its old-fashioned populist premise is that businessmen are rogues and guilty until proved innocent.
Feng started Erly.com with the premise that classifying our friends is actually an incredibly awkward exercise.
